metadata.title: The Wrong Job: Why the Silent SOC Mismatch Is the Costliest Error
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL=#1F6F5C=correct SOC/confirmed match/right wages, CRIMSON=#BF3339=wrong SOC/silent mismatch/mispriced role, GOLD=#F5D061=fill highlight only never text
metadata.note: Exclusions: no OEWS survey methodology, no job zone detail, no BLS pipeline build instructions — just the confirm/reject check and its consequences. No SOC taxonomy history. No BLS data collection mechanics.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.name: Title
beats.narration_text: A candidate gets role quality data: stable field, well-paid, growing. The data is describing a different job.

beats.name: ColdScene
beats.narration_text: A posting lands. Title: Growth Analyst. The system assigns a code. Numbers flow: median salary, employment trend, occupation profile. Everything looks real.

props.headline: A posting lands. Title: Growth Analyst. The system assigns a code.…

beats.scene_description: Person at desk reviewing a job posting on screen with data tables visible, editorial collage on aged newsprint
beats.image_prompt: B02, professional at laptop desk reviewing a job posting on screen with salary data and charts visible, editorial collage aesthetic on aged newsprint, desaturated tones, flat print reproduction, overhead angle, single desk lamp

beats.name: TheQuestion
beats.narration_text: Mapping a title should attach the right wages. Here is the case where the mapping is wrong and the wages are wrong — and nothing in the output says so. Why?

beats.name: TitleAmbiguity
beats.narration_text: Job titles are marketing artifacts. One company's Growth Analyst builds dashboards. Another's deploys machine learning models on product data. Same title, opposite work.

beats.name: SOCIsPivot
beats.narration_text: The SOC code is the pivot. Get it right, and occupation wages and employment trends follow correctly. Get it wrong — silently — and the numbers are real but describe the wrong occupation.

beats.name: TheMapping
beats.narration_text: A model proposes a plausible code. Growth Analyst looks like Market Research Analyst. The proposed SOC: 13-1161. Alternate-title list: Market Research Analyst, Consumer Insights Analyst, Survey Researcher. No modeling. No ML anywhere.

beats.name: TheCheck
beats.narration_text: The alternate-title list is the only check. If the posting's title or described work does not appear there, the match is unconfirmed — regardless of how plausible the proposed code seemed.

beats.name: RejectAndRetry
beats.narration_text: Rejected. Try 15-2051: Data Scientists. Alternate titles include Machine Learning Engineer, Predictive Analytics Engineer. The posting's work is there. Confirmed.

beats.name: TheGap
beats.narration_text: National median: Market Research Analyst, sixty-eight thousand. Data Scientist, one hundred eight thousand. Forty thousand dollars. The gap is real. The wrong mapping attached the wrong wages — silently, with no error message.

beats.name: WalkThrough
beats.narration_text: Posting: build and deploy ML models on product usage data. Model proposes 13-1161. Open the alternate-title list: no ML, no modeling, no data science. Rejected. Try 15-2051: Machine Learning Engineer is there. Confirmed. Wages: sixty-eight versus one-oh-eight thousand. Illustrative — same mechanism.

beats.name: Endcard
beats.narration_text: The numbers were real. They described the wrong occupation. The only check is the alternate-title list. If the work is not there, the match is unconfirmed.

beats.name: OutroSeries
beats.narration_text: Part of the The Reallocation Engine series.
